compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Implemented in commit 74dd64bdb (Change-Id I0e8af6ed). Pure-intercept CLI rebar verify-commit-ticket (verify_commit.py) with EXPECTED_FORMAT single-source constant (CLI error + --help + docs, pinned by a doc-drift test); path-safe extraction (traversal guard); resolution via resolve_ticket_id (alias/full/short/Jira). Config verify.require_ticket_for_commit (env override) enabled in rebar.toml. CI require-ticket job in gerrit-verify.yaml (idempotent mount + retry + distinguishable infra error + REBAR_TRACKER_DIR) wired into needs -> Verified-1 on failure. Docs: commit-ticket-trailer.md + CONTRIBUTING + CLAUDE + config.md. Tests: 23 pass (test_verify_commit_ticket.py) + help-overview parity contract intact. Gates: ruff+format+mypy clean; full suite 3882 passed (sole failure is the deselected integration/live-jira test -> bug fb50-f26a-d6fc-44c7). Dogfood: HEAD passes its own gate (crusty-dryad-spray -> 263d). Decision: no first-element-only rule (documented).

compiled_state.description:
## Context / Why All code landing on `main` goes through Gerrit's two-vote gate (LLM-Review + Verified). We want every such commit associated with a rebar ticket, enforced as part of the **Verified (CI)** leg — the only enforcement point that both blocks submit AND can resolve an ID against the live store. A `rebar-ticket:` commit trailer is introduced by this work as the convention (no code parses one yet, so the first submitted commits must carry it). CI does not apply to specialized branches (the `tickets` branch never goes through Gerrit), so this gates only code changes to `main`. The mechanism is intended to be reusable, so it is documented as a first-class rebar convention. ## What Add a `rebar verify-commit-ticket` check that extracts a ticket reference from a commit message and resolves it against the authoritative store via the standard config store logic (`config.tracker_dir()` / `config.tickets_branch()`), then wire it as a fail-fast job in `gerrit-verify.yaml` so a missing/unresolvable ticket drives Verified-1. Accept alias, full ID, short ID, and Jira key (project prefix) — all handled by the existing `resolve_ticket_id`. ## Decisions - **Location:** canonical is the `rebar-ticket: ` trailer; a leading `:` subject token is also accepted. First-element-ONLY enforcement is NOT adopted: no performance gain (a single store lookup regardless of position) and no fragility gain (both are fixed-position parses), while it would constrain every subject and eat the 50-char budget. - **Single source of truth:** the expected-format wording lives in ONE Python constant (`EXPECTED_FORMAT`). The CLI error and `--help` print it; the golden help capture and a doc-drift test pin `docs/commit-ticket-trailer.md` to it, so they cannot diverge. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] New `src/rebar/_commands/verify_commit.py`: - `EXPECTED_FORMAT: str` — the shared expected-format/accepted-forms/example/fix text. - `extract_ticket_refs(message) -> list[str]` — pull candidates from the `rebar-ticket:` trailer (case-insensitive; value tokens split on whitespace and stripped of `()`,`,`) and from a leading `:` subject token. **SECURITY:** drop any candidate that is not a single safe token — reject anything with `/`, `\`, `..`, whitespace, or control chars (require `^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9._-]{0,127}$`) BEFORE any store lookup, so a crafted `rebar-ticket: ../../etc/passwd` can never reach the resolver's `os.path.isdir(tracker/ )` fast path. Shape-matching (full/short/alias/Jira/prefix) stays in `resolve_ticket_id`; do NOT duplicate its `_FULL_ID_RE`/`_SHORT_ID_RE`/`_JIRA_KEY_RE` (avoids drift — a plausible-but-wrong inline shape would silently diverge). - `verify_commit_message(message, *, root=None) -> (ok: bool, tried: list[str], detail)` — resolve each safe candidate via `resolve_ticket_id` against `config.tracker_dir(root)`; ok if ANY resolves. - [ ] CLI `rebar verify-commit-ticket` (intercept in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py`, mirroring the `review-plan` intercept). Input is ONE of `--rev ` (reads via `git show -s --format=%B`), `--message-file

`, or `--message ` (mutually exclusive; default `--rev HEAD`). Behavior: - `verify.require_ticket_for_commit` false → exit 0 (no-op / staged rollout). - Merge commit (>1 parent, when `--rev` given) → exit 0 (exempt). - A safe candidate resolves → exit 0. - Else exit 1, printing `EXPECTED_FORMAT`: the `rebar-ticket: ` trailer format, the leading `:` alternative, the accepted ID forms (alias/full/short/Jira `-`), a worked example, the candidate(s) tried (or `(none present)`) with "none resolved", and the fix (add the trailer, `git commit --amend`, re-push). - I/O robustness: a bad `--rev` / missing `--message-file` / unreadable-or-absent store exits non-zero with a DISTINGUISHABLE error naming the cause (git failure, missing file, store not found) — never conflated with the missing-ticket diagnostic (so CI infra errors are told apart from a genuine ticket-less commit). - [ ] Registration = PURE INTERCEPT (matching `review-plan` / `verify-completion`): the arm in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` `main()` fires before `_dispatch` and owns its OWN argparse `--help` (which prints usage + `EXPECTED_FORMAT`). Intercepts carry NO `help/.txt`, NO `overview.txt` line, NO `tests/golden/cli_help/` files, and are NOT in the coverage test's `individual` set — they are invisible to the pinned-help parity contract (`test_routable_set_matches_pinned_help_set`), so none of those touchpoints apply. (Verified: review-plan/verify-completion/workflow follow exactly this pattern.) - [ ] Config: `verify.require_ticket_for_commit: bool = False` in `VerifyConfig` + `_SECTIONS['verify']` in `src/rebar/config.py` (env `REBAR_VERIFY_REQUIRE_TICKET_FOR_COMMIT` via the generic env layer); enabled for THIS project in `rebar.toml`. - [ ] Store resolution uses the standard config helpers `config.tracker_dir()` / `config.tickets_branch()` and is overridable via `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` (relocates the store, highest precedence) / `tracker.dir` / `tracker.branch` — no bespoke store path. (Local resolution reads the mounted store files; the CI job separately mounts that store — see below.) - [ ] CI: `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` gains a `require-ticket` job (needs `clear-vote`; `build-and-test` + `vote` gain it in `needs` so a failure fails the run → Verified-1, fail-fast before the matrix). Runners are GitHub-hosted + ephemeral (fresh workspace per run); fetch+worktree is used (over per-file `git show tickets:path`) because the standard resolver needs a real tracker dir to read. Steps: - checkout the Gerrit change (`checkout-gerrit-change-action`) + `pip install -e .`; - mount the authoritative tickets store from GitHub, **idempotently and with retry**: `git worktree prune` + `rm -rf .tickets-tracker` first; then up to 3 attempts of `timeout 60 git fetch --depth=1 https://github.com/${{ github.repository }} '+tickets:refs/remotes/gh-tickets'` (short sleep-backoff between attempts); then `git worktree add --detach .tickets-tracker gh-tickets`. On persistent fetch failure, print a DISTINGUISHABLE infra-error (not a missing-ticket error) advising `recheck`, and fail closed (two-vote fail-closed model); - `export REBAR_TRACKER_DIR="$PWD/.tickets-tracker"` (pin the store explicitly rather than rely on the default name) and run `rebar verify-commit-ticket --rev HEAD` (the CLI reads the message itself from git; no untrusted commit text is interpolated onto a command line). - [ ] Tests `tests/unit/test_verify_commit_ticket.py`: - extraction: trailer with parenthetical, leading `:`, multiple, none, AND a path-traversal candidate is dropped (security); - resolution against a fixture store for full / short / alias / prefix, and a Jira key via a `.bridge_state/bindings.json` fixture; - config-gate off → exit 0; enabled + no ticket → exit 1 AND the diagnostic contains the trailer format + accepted forms + an example (asserted against `EXPECTED_FORMAT`); - merge-commit exempt; the three input modes; a valid leading-`:` commit → exit 0. - [ ] Doc-drift test: a unit test imports `EXPECTED_FORMAT` and asserts `docs/commit-ticket-trailer.md` contains its text, so docs cannot silently drift. - [ ] Docs (accurate + discoverable; a reusable, rebar-enforced convention for BOTH clients and contributors): - `CONTRIBUTING.md` — every commit to `main` must carry a resolvable ticket ref; the trailer format, the leading-`:` alternative, accepted ID forms, CI enforcement. - `CLAUDE.md` — in the `## Git workflow (code changes)` section, the same for agents. - `docs/config.md` — the `verify.require_ticket_for_commit` flag. - `docs/commit-ticket-trailer.md` (new) — the reusable mechanism: trailer convention + `rebar verify-commit-ticket` + extraction/resolution + config; linked from the two above. ## Verification - `pytest tests/unit/test_verify_commit_ticket.py -q` - `pytest tests/interfaces/contracts/test_help_overview_coverage.py -q` (intercept leaves parity intact) - `rebar verify-commit-ticket --message "x: no ticket"; echo $?` → 1, printing EXPECTED_FORMAT - `rebar verify-commit-ticket --message-file <(git show -s --format=%B HEAD); echo $?` → 0 - `grep -l 'rebar-ticket:' CONTRIBUTING.md CLAUDE.md docs/config.md docs/commit-ticket-trailer.md` (all four document the trailer) - CI-path (`require-ticket` job) is proved by an operator throwaway change, like the two-vote E2E. ## Out of scope - Non-Gerrit paths (the `tickets` branch; direct GitHub pushes — already blocked by the ruleset). - An advisory client-side `commit-msg` hook (optional future nicety). - A `rebar-ticket: none` bypass sentinel (kept strict; merge commits are the only exemption).

file_impact.reason: enable the gate for this project
Git workflow section: required ticket reference

compiled_state.title: CI Verified gate: require a resolvable rebar ticket ID in every Gerrit commit to main
